records.ety: [L. contradictus, p. p. of contradicere to speak against; contra + dicere to speak. See Diction.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Contradicted; p. pr. & vb. n. Contradicting.]

defs: To assert the contrary of; to oppose in words; to take issue with; to gainsay; to deny the truth of, as of a statement or a speaker; to impugn.
quotes: Dear Duff, I prithee, contradict thyself, And say it is not so.
The future can not contradict the past.

defs: To be contrary to; to oppose; to resist.
quotes: No truth can contradict another truth.
A greater power than we can contradict Hath thwarted our intents.

defs: To oppose in words; to gainsay; to deny, or assert the contrary of, something.
quotes: They . . . spake against those things which were spoken by Paul, contradicting and blaspheming.
